rationale: The citation is not just background; it is tied to a concrete methodological choice in the empirical design. The authors explicitly state that their spare-money variable is measured using three items from Berman et al. (2016, Study 3). That borrowed measure is then used in discriminant-validity and predictive analyses, giving the cited paper a meaningful applied role even though it is not the main theoretical foundation of the article.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Berman et al. (2016) as the source for a three-item spare-money measure in Study 1. It applies that prior measurement instrument to control for perceived financial slack when testing psychological ownership of borrowed money.
